cards.title: Oral-Systemic Connection — Overview

alerts.label: The Mouth as a Window to Systemic Health
alerts.text: The oral cavity is intimately connected to systemic health through: shared microbiomes (oral → gut axis), systemic inflammatory load (periodontal disease → ↑ CRP, IL-6 → cardiovascular disease, diabetes complications), shared developmental embryology (oral and cardiac structures share neural crest origin), and as a diagnostic window (nutritional deficiencies, autoimmune disease, cancer, diabetes, HIV all manifest orally first).

rows: Periodontal Disease
Cardiovascular disease, T2DM, adverse pregnancy outcomes, Alzheimer's, RA, COPD
Gram-negative anaerobes → bacteraemia → systemic inflammation (CRP ↑, IL-6 ↑, fibrinogen ↑) + molecular mimicry (Pg antibodies cross-react with cardiac tissue)
★★★ Periodontal treatment reduces HbA1c by 0.4% in T2DM (comparable to adding second antidiabetic drug)
Oral Dysbiosis
Colorectal cancer (Fusobacterium nucleatum), IBD, pancreatic cancer
Fusobacterium migrates to colorectal tumours — promotes tumour growth via E-cadherin/Wnt signalling. Found in 90% of CRC tumours
★★★ F. nucleatum abundance in stool correlates with CRC risk — emerging diagnostic marker
Tooth Loss
Dementia, malnutrition, cardiovascular disease, frailty
Reduced chewing → ↓ hippocampal stimulation (jaw movement activates memory circuits), dietary restriction → nutritional deficiency, social isolation
★★★ Edentulous adults have 2× dementia risk — especially when tooth loss occurs before 65
Obstructive Sleep Apnoea (OSA)
Hypertension, T2DM, atrial fibrillation, stroke, depression
Intermittent hypoxia → sympathetic activation, oxidative stress, endothelial dysfunction → cardiovascular cascade
★★★ OSA: 2× cardiovascular risk, 3× hypertension risk. Mandibular advancement devices — effective OSA treatment (dental appliances)

cards.title: Naturopathic Oral Hygiene & Microbiome

steps.title: Oil Pulling (Kavala / Gandush)
steps.text: Traditional Ayurvedic practice with growing evidence. Swish 1 tablespoon sesame or coconut oil in mouth for 10–20 minutes (do not swallow — contains accumulated bacteria). Spit into bin (not drain — clogs pipes). Rinse with warm saline. Perform before breakfast, before tooth brushing. Evidence: ★★★ Reduces Streptococcus mutans (caries bacteria) comparable to chlorhexidine mouthwash. Reduces plaque and gingivitis scores in RCTs. Coconut oil: additional antifungal effect (against Candida).

steps.title: Tongue Scraping
steps.text: Use copper or stainless steel tongue scraper on waking. Gentle strokes from posterior to anterior (5–10 strokes). Removes: ama (Ayurvedic — undigested metabolic waste), bacteria, volatile sulphur compounds (VSCs — primary cause of halitosis). Evidence: significant reduction in VSCs and halitosis ★★. Copper tongue scraper: additional antimicrobial benefit from copper ions. Tongue coating assessment is an important naturopathic diagnostic tool (thick white = gut dysbiosis/candida; yellow = excess heat/liver burden).

steps.title: Oral Probiotic Therapy
steps.text: Streptococcus salivarius K12 (BLIS K12) ★★★ — reduces pharyngitis frequency, inhibits pathogenic oral bacteria. Lactobacillus reuteri DSM 17938 ★★ — reduces gingival bleeding, plaque, and gingivitis. Lactobacillus acidophilus — reduces caries-causing S. mutans. Protocol: lozenge formulations preferred over capsules for oral cavity colonisation. Use after antibiotic course to restore oral microbiome. Avoid: immediately after antiseptic mouthwash (kills probiotic bacteria).

steps.title: Herbal Oral Care
steps.text: Neem (Azadirachta indica) chew sticks or toothpaste ★★★ — comparable to chlorhexidine for plaque reduction. Anti-Streptococcus, antibiofilm. Clove oil (eugenol) — analgesic, antimicrobial, antifungal. Dilute (5% in carrier oil) for direct application to mouth ulcers or dental pain. Myrrh (Commiphora) — antiseptic, wound healing, oral mucositis. Calendula — oral ulcer healing. Propolis (bee resin) ★★ — broad-spectrum antimicrobial, promotes oral wound healing, reduces plaque (comparable to triclosan).

cards.title: Common Oral Conditions — Naturopathic Management

rows: Dental Caries (Prevention)
S. mutans colonisation + dietary sugar → acid dissolution of enamel

Xylitol 6–10g/day (inhibits S. mutans ★★★ — use xylitol gum after meals). Reduce sugar frequency (not just amount — acid attack duration matters). Oil pulling. Green tea catechins (anti-S. mutans). Calcium + Vit D for enamel remineralisation. Fluoride: evidence-based conventional adjunct (naturopathic practitioners may prefer alternatives — xylitol + remineralisation pastes)
Established caries: dental referral for filling/treatment. Abscesses: urgent dental referral
Gingivitis / Periodontitis
Gum inflammation (gingivitis — reversible) → bone loss (periodontitis — irreversible without treatment)

CoQ10 100–300mg/day ★★★ (gingival tissue has highest CoQ10 density — deficiency → periodontitis). Vit C 500mg–1g/day (collagen synthesis for periodontal ligament). Vit D 2000IU/day ★★★ (immunomodulatory — reduces pocket depth). Folic acid rinse ★★. Neem toothpaste. Oil pulling. Professional cleaning (SRP) is essential — naturopathy is adjunct
Pocket depth >4mm, bone loss on X-ray, tooth mobility: periodontist referral essential
Aphthous Ulcers (Canker Sores)
Painful oral ulcers 3–10mm, grey base, erythematous halo, recurrent

Vit B12 1000mcg/day ★★★ (RCT: reduces frequency, duration, pain even in non-deficient patients). Zinc 30mg/day ★★. Iron + folate if deficient. Identify triggers: SLS (sodium lauryl sulphate in toothpaste — switch to SLS-free), food triggers (nuts, tomato, chocolate). Aloe vera gel topical ★★. Liquorice root (DGL) — promotes mucosal healing
Recurrent major ulcers (>1cm), failure to heal >3 weeks: rule out Behçet's, Crohn's, coeliac, haematological malignancy
Oral Candidiasis (Thrush)
White plaques (removable) on buccal mucosa, tongue. Common in: immunosuppressed, denture wearers, inhaled corticosteroid users, post-antibiotics

Coconut oil pulling (caprylic acid — antifungal), diluted tea tree oil rinse (0.5% — antifungal), probiotic Lactobacillus (repopulate normal flora), Vit D (immune support), xylitol (reduces Candida adhesion), caprylic acid 1–2g with meals. Remove trigger (antibiotics if possible, clean dentures, spacer for inhaled steroids)
Immunocompromised patients: fluconazole referral. Oesophageal involvement (dysphagia): urgent referral
Bruxism (Teeth Grinding)
Nocturnal grinding/clenching → enamel wear, TMJ pain, headache, disrupted sleep

Magnesium glycinate 400mg before bed ★★ (muscle relaxant, reduces nocturnal bruxism frequency). B vitamins (stress-related bruxism). Mindfulness + stress reduction ★★. Yoga Nidra for sleep relaxation. Address underlying anxiety/sleep disorders. Occlusal splint (dental — protects teeth): refer to dentist
Significant enamel wear, TMJ dysfunction, trismus: dental/maxillofacial referral

cards.title: Dental Anxiety & Oral Health in Special Populations

rows: Pregnancy
Gingivitis in 40–70% of pregnancies (progesterone-driven vascular changes). Pregnancy epulis. Morning sickness → enamel erosion. Dental treatment safe in 2nd trimester.

Rinsing with water + baking soda after vomiting (neutralises acid before tooth erosion occurs — wait 30 min before brushing). Calcium 1000mg + Vit D 2000IU. Probiotic for oral microbiome. Periodontal treatment reduces preterm birth risk ★★.
Elderly
Dry mouth (xerostomia) from polypharmacy (100+ drugs cause it). Root caries. Denture-related candidiasis. Reduced enamel remineralisation.

Xylitol lozenges for dry mouth (stimulates saliva). Oral hydration. Saliva substitutes (naturopathic: aloe vera gel as mucosal coating). Vit D + calcium for jaw bone density. Review polypharmacy with physician (reduce xerogenic drugs where possible).
Diabetes
Bidirectional relationship: DM → ↑ periodontitis severity (3× risk). Periodontitis → ↑ HbA1c (inflammatory load). Treatment of periodontitis reduces HbA1c ★★★.
Periodontal care as integral part of diabetes management. Vit D, CoQ10, zinc for oral immunity. Control blood glucose (naturopathic DM protocol) — directly reduces periodontal disease activity.
Eating Disorders
Bulimia → severe enamel erosion (lingual surfaces), parotid swelling, loss of tooth structure. Anorexia → bone density loss (including jaw).

Supportive, non-judgemental approach. Rinsing with baking soda water after purging (reduces acid erosion). Calcium + Vit D. Refer: eating disorder team + gastroenterologist + dentist collaboratively. Fluoride varnish (dental) to protect remaining enamel.

pearls.label: 🟡 Mercury Amalgam — Clinical Guidance
pearls.text: Silver amalgam fillings contain 50% mercury by weight. WHO and EU have moved toward phasing out dental amalgam. Biological dentists offer mercury-safe removal using SMART protocol (Smoke Evacuation, Mercury separator, Amalgam pieces in large chunks, Rubber dam, Tablet of activated charcoal before/after). Naturopathic support during and after amalgam removal: activated charcoal 2g 30 min before appointment (binds swallowed mercury particles), chlorella 5g/day, vitamin C 2g/day, NAC 600mg BD (glutathione support). Note: amalgam-related mercury toxicity is controversial — most studies show blood mercury levels within safe range in people with amalgam fillings. Refer to biological dentist for patient-centred decision-making.
